The inequality representing the requirement that the button's area is at least 10 square inches is $2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 \ge 10$, which simplifies to $2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 17 \ge 0$. To determine the valid range of $x$ between 3 and 6, we evaluate the function at the endpoints and check for roots. At $x=3$, $P(3) = 2(27) - 5(9) + 3(3) - 7 = 54 - 45 + 9 - 7 = 11$, which satisfies the condition since $11 \ge 10$. At $x=4$, $P(4) = 2(64) - 5(16) + 3(4) - 7 = 128 - 80 + 12 - 7 = 53$, which is also greater than 10. Since the derivative $P'(x) = 6x^2 - 10x + 3$ is positive for all $x \ge 3$ (the roots of the derivative are approximately 0.3 and 1.3), the function is strictly increasing in the interval $[3, 6]$. Therefore, if the condition holds at the minimum value $x=3$, it holds for the entire interval. The range of $x$-values satisfying the condition within the specified domain is $3 \le x \le 6$.
